expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves component-stack constraints, embedding lifecycle assumptions, and integration-sensitive customer expectations
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly structures the intake around the three required focus areas: (1) "Component / stack constraints that must be preserved in intake" citing labels "Embedding_SDK" and "Library_and_Dashboards" and the "REST API Doc" hint; (2) "Embedding lifecycle assumptions to carry forward" citing native-embedding and the parent initiative and noting lifecycle coverage; (3) "Integration-sensitive customer expectations" citing "CVS Pharmacy, Inc. — CS0928640" and "customer_signal_present: true". Although it marks overall coverage as PARTIAL due to missing manifest, the case focus is explicitly covered in the content.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: result.md is explicitly framed as "Phase 1 Context Intake Contract Check" and stays within Phase 1 responsibilities (spawn-manifest generation and evidence routing), including a dedicated section "Phase 1 contract alignment" and noting "Phase 1 output is phase1_spawn_manifest.json". It also states: "Phase alignment: PASS (this artifact stays within Phase 1 contract/intake expectations; no later-phase drafting claims)."
